close_reason: Fixed: validate_key_type() checks TPM key type against URI algorithm in public_key_der()
description: public_key_der() does not validate that the TPM key's actual algorithm matches the algorithm specified in the URI. If a user configures ?algorithm=ecdsa-p256 but the persistent handle points to an RSA-2048 key, algorithm() returns EcdsaP256Sha256, sign() correctly returns AlgorithmMismatch (via normalize_signature), but public_key_der() silently returns an RSA SPKI. A caller doing algorithm() + public_key_der() before sign() would see inconsistent results: the algorithm says ECDSA but the SPKI is RSA. Fix: after read_public(), inspect the Public variant (Rsa vs Ecc) and compare against self.algorithm. Return AlgorithmMismatch early if they disagree.
title: public_key_der ignores algorithm mismatch
